name: KMP Platform-Bridge Extractor
short_description: Extracts the full Kotlin Multiplatform platform-bridging surface: expect/actual declaration pairs, @ObjCName annotations, SKIE-enhanced Swift API shapes, ObjC header exports, and Swift Export (experimental, Kotlin 2.2.x). Produces a bridge-surface graph enabling impact analysis when shared API changes.
tech_stack: tree-sitter-kotlin — AST extraction of expect/actual keywords, @ObjCName/@ObjCProtocolName annotations, @HiddenFromObjC

Kotlin compiler plugin (compiler-embeddable) or kotlin-analysis-api — programmatic access to resolved expect/actual linkage, platform target metadata; more accurate than tree-sitter for multi-round type inference
SKIE Gradle plugin (Touchlab, v0.x) — generates Swift overlay declarations; SKIE API report JSON can be parsed post-build
swift-syntax (SwiftPM) or SourceKit-LSP — parse generated .swiftinterface files to extract Swift-visible API surface
objc-header-parser (custom, or libclang) — parse generated ObjC umbrella header (.h) to extract @interface/@protocol declarations
custom Python/Kotlin driver — orchestrates extraction, aligns expect<->actual pairs, emits bridge surface to Graphiti
entity_model: Produces :ExpectDeclaration nodes (fqn, file_path, kind=[class|function|object|property], target_platforms=[android|ios|jvm|js]) and :ActualDeclaration nodes (fqn, file_path, platform, implementation_notes). Edge :IMPLEMENTS links ActualDeclaration → ExpectDeclaration. Produces :ObjCExport nodes (kotlin_fqn, objc_name, swift_name, export_kind=[class|protocol|function|property], is_skie_enhanced=bool, has_objc_name_annotation=bool). :EXPOSES edge links :ObjCExport → :ExpectDeclaration or :ActualDeclaration. For Swift Export path (Kotlin 2.2.x experimental): :SwiftExportSymbol nodes with swift_module, swift_name. All bridge entities apply structural facet structural=platform-bridge, capability=KMPBridge. :BridgeSurfaceSnapshot node (timestamp, kotlin_version, skie_version) acts as snapshot root for temporal queries.
storage_format: graph-node — primary storage in Graphiti for Cypher traversal. Supplementary .knowledge/kmp-bridge-surface.md snapshot (human-readable table of expect/actual pairs + ObjC export names) written per ingest cycle. Rationale: bridge surface is critical for iOS engineer orientation; markdown provides stable non-graph access.

prior_art.name: SKIE — Swift Kotlin Interface Enhancer
prior_art.description: Gradle plugin by Touchlab that enhances ObjC-generated Swift API (sealed class → Swift enum, suspend → async/throws, Flow → AsyncSequence). Produces structured API change reports. Does not yet support Swift Export path. Actively maintained 2024–2025.

prior_art.name: Kotlin Swift Export (JetBrains roadmap)
prior_art.description: JetBrains Aug 2025 roadmap: Swift Export experimental in Kotlin 2.2.x, target stable 2026. Generates pure Swift without ObjC layer. SKIE does not support this path yet. Breaking change from ObjC-export paradigm.

prior_art.name: Kotlin ObjC Interop documentation
prior_art.description: Canonical reference for @ObjCName, @ObjCProtocolName, @HiddenFromObjC annotations and name mangling rules.

prior_art.name: KMMBridge (Touchlab)
prior_art.description: Framework for publishing KMP XCFrameworks to SPM/CocoaPods. Integrates SKIE. Used with PowerSync example. Provides build pipeline reference for bridge surface publication.

prior_art.name: swift-api-digester / diagnose-api-breaking-changes
prior_art.description: Built into Swift toolchain. Detects breaking changes in .swiftinterface files between versions. Relevant for tracking KMP bridge surface breakage from Kotlin side changes. Known issues with ObjC dependencies (Issue #75847).
prior_art.maintenance: maintained as part of Swift toolchain

prior_art.name: scip-kotlin (Sourcegraph)
prior_art.description: SCIP indexer for Kotlin via SemanticDB compiler plugin. Could provide stable SCIP symbol IDs for expect/actual fqns. Less mature than scip-java Java support.

prior_art.name: Kotlin binary-compatibility-validator
prior_art.description: JetBrains tool generating .api dump files per module, now with experimental KLib ABI support (2024). apiCheck task detects breaking public API changes. Directly applicable to validating expect/actual contract stability.

graphiti_episode_type: EntityNode — :ExpectDeclaration and :ActualDeclaration are permanent structural nodes until declaration is removed (carry end_time). :ObjCExport nodes carry end_time when @ObjCName annotation or SKIE configuration changes. :BridgeSurfaceSnapshot is an EpisodicNode with a point-in-time validity.
ingest_perf_est: [uncertain] Tree-sitter pass for expect/actual extraction on 200K LOC Kotlin: ~1–3 minutes. Annotation extraction: fast (~30s). ObjC header parsing (libclang or custom): ~1–2 minutes for a 20-library XCFramework. SKIE API report parsing: ~10–30s (post-build artifact). Full ingest including Kotlin compiler plugin invocation: ~5–15 minutes (Kotlin compilation is the bottleneck). Incremental: ~1–3 minutes for a changed module. All estimates based on analogous KMP build times, not direct measurement.
failure_modes: SKIE does not support Swift Export path (as of 2025) — when Kotlin migrates to Swift Export, the ObjC-header-based extraction pipeline becomes obsolete and must be rewritten for .swiftinterface parsing
Swift Export is experimental in Kotlin 2.2.x — schema designed today may need significant revision once Swift Export stabilises (target 2026)
diagnose-api-breaking-changes fails with 'redefinition of module' errors in some configurations (Swift Forums Issue #8103) — reduces reliability of ObjC-header diff pipeline
@ObjCName annotation is still marked experimental as of Kotlin 2.x — name mangling rules may change across compiler versions
Generated SKIE Swift overlay code in build/ may not be preserved between CI runs — requires build step before extraction, coupling ingest to compilation
open_questions: SKIE vs Swift Export migration timing: should the extractor schema model both ObjC-export and Swift-Export paths in parallel, accepting dual-path complexity, or delay implementation until Swift Export stabilises (2026)? This is a fundamental schema fork decision.

Overlap with Item 27 (Public API Surface Extractor): ObjCExport nodes overlap with exported symbol surface. Should bridge-surface nodes be merged with Item 27 output or kept separate with :EXPOSES edges? Merging simplifies the graph; separation preserves semantic clarity.

How should expect/actual pairs where the actual is platform-specific (iosMain, androidMain) be distinguished from expect/actual pairs where actual is in a common non-shared module? Relevant for Unstoppable Wallet's Kit architecture.

KMMBridge XCFramework publishing: should the extractor track which Kotlin version produced a given bridge snapshot to enable version-skew analysis across Kit libraries? Requires versioning :BridgeSurfaceSnapshot nodes.

Compiler plugin approach (kotlin-analysis-api) vs tree-sitter: the compiler plugin gives 100% accurate multi-platform resolution but requires a full Kotlin compilation pass — is this acceptable for incremental ingest latency targets?
adversarial_resistance: high — expect/actual keywords and @ObjCName annotations are syntactic; not obfuscatable at source level. Generated ObjC headers are compiler output and deterministic given the same source.
